audit_items.proof_trace: The function `ldap_create_vlv_control` checks only `ctrlp == NULL` (L215) but immediately dereferences `ld` at L216 (`ld->ld_errno = LDAP_PARAM_ERROR`) and L220 without any NULL guard on `ld`. The sibling function `ldap_create_vlv_control_value` (L94) correctly guards with `if ( ld == NULL || ... )` before any dereference, proving the library authors intended to handle NULL `ld` — making this omission an inconsistency and a real null pointer dereference defect.
audit_items.attack_scenario: A caller invoking `ldap_create_vlv_control(NULL, vlvinfop, ctrlp)` — e.g., a server-side component that fails to properly initialize the LDAP session handle before processing a VLV search request — crashes at L216 or L220, enabling remote denial of service.
